title: alsine rosanoi var. subglabra
intro: Alsine rosanoi var. subglabra, also known as Alsine, is a plant species that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly referred to as the pink or carnation family. Alsine is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and diuretic effects, making it a natural remedy for issues such as urinary tract infections and digestive problems. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. Alsine can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for topical use, and its extracts have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being.
